meta.title: Mission Control
meta.vintages: KS4 attainment 2024/25 (LAD workbooks); KS2 2024/25 revised; absence 2024/25; 16-17 NEET 2025 (Dec 2024-Feb 2025 avg); KS4 destinations 2022/23 cohort; IDACI 2025.

sunderland.name: Sunderland
sunderland.summary: Phase 1 Mission NE cluster. Sustained below-average KS4 attainment, among the highest disadvantaged underperformance in the region, elevated persistent absence, high deprivation (53rd most deprived nationally on IDACI), and no existing targeted DfE support (RISE) — genuine additionality.
sunderland.composite_rank_ne: 2nd weakest of 12 NE LAs on the combined ranking (score 53)

south-tyneside.name: South Tyneside
south-tyneside.summary: Phase 1 Mission NE cluster. A8 of 42.7 vs NE average 44.1 and national 46.0; disadvantaged A8 33.1, nearly two points below the national average; elevated persistent absence; 48th most deprived nationally on IDACI; not covered by RISE or other targeted programmes.
south-tyneside.composite_rank_ne: Joint 2nd weakest of 12 NE LAs on the combined ranking (score 53)

scarborough.name: Scarborough
scarborough.summary: Mission Coastal (north). Chosen by Ministers from a data-led shortlist of coastal LADs. Disadvantaged A8 of 26.6 is among the weakest nationally. KS2 is notably weak: over half of primaries below national average, so the mission includes a strong primary attainment focus. Strong trusts (e.g. Delta) have recently taken on weak secondaries but pupil engagement remains the core challenge. Former Opportunity Area and Priority Education Investment Area.
scarborough.idaciNote: LAD-level IDACI not published on the same basis; district contains some of the most deprived coastal neighbourhoods in Yorkshire.
scarborough.neetNote: 16-17 NEET is only published at LA level: figures shown are North Yorkshire, which will understate the Scarborough picture.

hastings.name: Hastings
hastings.summary: Mission Coastal (south). Disadvantaged A8 of 26.0 — the lowest of any coastal area in England for three years running. Defining challenge is churn: high pupil mobility driven by housing insecurity, plus a very large elective home education cohort (~10% of secondary-age pupils). Former Opportunity Area and PEIA.
hastings.neetNote: 16-17 NEET is only published at LA level: figures shown are East Sussex, which will understate the Hastings picture.

hastings.programmes: Opportunity Area, PEIA, RISE at 1 secondary.

patterns.title: Deprivation does not explain MCS performance
patterns.text: Across the rest of North Yorkshire disadvantage strongly predicts results (correlation -0.43). Within MCS it is essentially zero (-0.05). Some of the most deprived schools are the best performers (Airy Hill 45% disadvantaged, 85% RWM; Thomas Hinderwell 50%, 77%). The problem is school-level, not demographic — fixable, with local proof points.

patterns.title: The girls’ advantage has vanished
patterns.text: Elsewhere in the county girls outperform boys by 6-7pp on RWM. In MCS schools girls achieve 58% vs 66% for girls elsewhere (a 7.7pp gap, vs 2.9pp for boys). At Lindhead only 7% of girls reached the expected standard against 35% of boys.

patterns.title: Reading is strong; maths blocks the combined measure
patterns.text: Several schools post strong reading but weak RWM (Fylingdales reads at 93% yet only 40% achieve RWM). Maths is the binding constraint in most cases, writing in a few.

hastingsCohort.note: Hastings postcode area, Years 7-11, mid-2026. Secondary-age children out of, or at risk of being out of, provision.

strands.name: Intensive School Improvement
strands.text: Performance Support Teams of experienced leaders and specialists from high-performing trusts deployed into clusters of 6-8 secondary schools, working alongside local staff on attendance, behaviour, reading and KS3 catch-up. Practitioner-led, not consultancy.

strands.name: Pupil Engagement & Enrichment
strands.text: A structured enrichment and mentoring offer for every child in the mission areas, coordinated with the national Enrichment Framework and the DCMS £22.5m investment: sport, arts, careers, mentoring, work experience, delivered with anchor partners Right to Succeed, AllChild and Impetus.

strands.name: Test, Learn & Grow
strands.text: Place-based TLG projects, micro-RCTs and multi-agency pilots, scoped bottom-up with local partners and supported by a Cabinet Office-funded satellite team. The missions as the R&D arm of the English school system.

strands.name: Leadership Capacity
strands.text: Building the capacity and development of school leaders: place-led, focused on disadvantaged communities, with a long-term pipeline (Leadership Programmes North East plus philanthropic match).

lines.note: ~25 specialists @ £800/day, ~120 days each, clusters of 8-12 schools
One experienced educationalist per mission on secondment
PM, two programme officers, admin, board secretariat
£250k per mission: backfill, TLG project leads, innovation pots
Top-up to Pupil Engagement Framework and Enrichment Framework rollout
Real-time dashboard, impact evaluation, research partnership
Leadership Programmes North East + philanthropic match

tlg.name: National Rapid Evidence Network
tlg.text: Micro-trial infrastructure in mission schools growing into an opt-in rapid evidence network any school in England can join.

tlg.name: Child Engagement Early Warning System
tlg.text: Join DfE attendance with NHS, social care and youth service signals into a single safeguarding-grade view per child, with predictive flags tested at the Y6-7 transition and a tested human response paired to every flag.

tlg.name: Family Engagement Guarantee
tlg.text: Trials of structured parental engagement: text-nudges, community parent mentors, family learning, reverse parents’ evenings — targeting white working-class families reporting the lowest trust in schools.

tlg.name: Enrichment Passport & outcome-linked commissioning
tlg.text: A digital enrichment passport for every mission pupil; providers paid partly on verified participation of disadvantaged pupils.

tlg.name: The Year 7 Bridge
tlg.text: The Y6-7 transition as a designed experience: summer bridge on secondary sites, one childhood one record, KS3 engagement curricula, transition mentors from Y9-10.

tlg.name: Inclusion Labs
tlg.text: Volunteer mission schools test inclusive mainstream models the SEND reform programme needs evidence for.

tlg.name: KS3 Micro-Trial Partnership
tlg.text: Deepen the WhatWorked Education partnership into the country’s first secondary classroom-practice trial unit embedded in PST cluster schools; every PST intervention becomes a trial arm.

tlg.name: “Made in the North East” industry pathways
tlg.text: Employer-embedded enrichment at scale using advanced manufacturing, electrification, offshore energy and Crown Works Studios: workplace encounters from Y7, employer-sponsored KS3 projects, technical tasters.

tlg.name: “Every Mover Known”
tlg.text: Rapid-integration protocol for mid-year movers (48-hour records transfer, automatic enrichment and mentoring enrolment, named integration lead) plus a coastal workforce premium trial.

tlg.name: “The Year-Round Coast”
tlg.text: Year-round opportunity model for a seasonal economy: holiday enrichment, paid summer roles with tourism/offshore wind/marine sectors, a coastal curriculum, school-year scheduling flexibilities.

vmostSeed.vision: Every child achieving and thriving — a country where the place a child grows up, from the North East to the coast, no longer sets a ceiling on how far they go.
vmostSeed.mission: Place-based missions in the North East and coastal towns: a whole-government commitment to children, inside the school gates and beyond them — practitioner-led, starting where need is greatest. Focus areas: South Tyneside, Sunderland, Hastings, Scarborough.

objectives.title: Narrow the gap
objectives.detail: Narrow the 17.7-point White British FSM attainment gap against pre-mission baselines.

strategies.title: Efficacy engine
strategies.detail: Teacher × pupil × parental efficacy connected by culture, relationships and accountability.

tactics.title: PSTs deployed to clusters of 6-8 secondaries
tactics.detail: Specialists from high-performing trusts working on attendance, behaviour, reading, KS3 catch-up.

objectives.title: Raise attainment
objectives.detail: Lift Attainment 8 towards the England average (46.0). NE 44.1; Hastings disadvantaged 26.0.

strategies.title: One integrated model
strategies.detail: Four strands delivered together, centred on place.

tactics.title: Shared reading-fluency app + KS3 early intervention
tactics.detail: Across the cohort in cluster schools.

objectives.title: Cut absence
objectives.detail: Reduce overall and persistent absence, tracked termly.

strategies.title: Engagement beyond the gate
strategies.detail: Attendance is a symptom; engagement of pupil, parent and community is the driver.

tactics.title: Daily attendance monitoring + family liaison
tactics.detail: Persistent-absence protocols in every cluster school.

objectives.title: Enrich
objectives.detail: Guaranteed enrichment participation for every pupil as a mission metric.

strategies.title: Collective impact partnership
strategies.detail: Anchor partners Right to Succeed, AllChild, Impetus + DCMS £22.5m.

tactics.title: Enrichment entitlement aligned with DCMS investment
tactics.detail: Sport, arts, careers, mentoring, work experience.

strategies.title: Phased scaling
strategies.detail: Plan summer 2026, deliver from Sept 2026, a new cluster each term: refine before we scale.
